item.title: Judge denies Fulton County's request to return 2020 ballots seized by FBI
item.description: A federal judge in Georgia denied a motion by officials in Fulton County, Georgia, to return ballots and other materials from the 2020 election that were taken by the FBI earlier this year.

item.title: What's next for those aboard hantavirus cruise ship?
item.description: Investigators are racing to control a deadly outbreak of hantavirus, the first ever recorded on a cruise ship. More than 140 people are still on board, including 17 Americans. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran strikes, says it's too soon for new direct talks
item.description: Trump threatens Iran with "higher level" strikes if it won't accept a peace deal, but says it's too soon for direct talks after reporting diplomatic progress.

item.title: Rep. Suzan DelBene responds to criticism about DCCC weighing in on contested primaries
item.description: The House Democrats' campaign arm is wading into more highly contested primary races across the country. In a rare rebuke, several House progressives, including Pramila Jayapal and Jamie Raskin, are calling on the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee to let voters decide. Democratic Rep. Suzan DelBene of Washington State, chair of the DCCC, joins "The Takeout" to respond.

item.title: Blanche: Obama's concerns about DOJ targeting Trump critics are "extraordinarily rich"
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche denied to CBS News that the Justice Department is engaging in a retribution campaign against President Trump's critics, pushing back on criticism from Barack Obama.

item.title: Todd Blanche says claims of Justice Department politicization are "simply false"
item.description: Critics have expressed concerns about President Trump's use of the Justice Department, including former President Barack Obama during a taped interview on "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert." CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez sat down for an exclusive interview with acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, who defended the Justice Department's actions.

item.title: California gubernatorial candidates describe Newsom's tenure in one word
item.description: Five California Democrats and two Republicans met on the debate stage yet again in the primary for governor Tuesday night. Political strategists Erin Maguire and Kendra Barkoff Lamy join "The Takeout" with analysis on the state of the race.

item.title: What Indiana, Ohio primary wins say about Trump's sway on voters
item.description: President Trump may not have been on the ballot in Indiana on Tuesday, but he won big in the primaries. The Associated Press reports five incumbent state Senators lost their races to Trump-backed candidates. One lawmaker won his primary and another's is too close to call. They all voted against the president's redistricting plan for Indiana last year. CBS News' Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: Food manufacturer Cento is committing "tomato fraud," lawsuit alleges
item.description: In a new lawsuit, two California residents allege that Cento Fine Foods doesn't use real San Marzano tomatoes in one of its products.

item.title: 3 evacuated from hantavirus cruise as Spain says it will dock in Tenerife
item.description: Three passengers have been evacuated from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak, as related cases are confirmed in Switzerland and South Africa.

item.title: What we know about Howard Lutnick's testimony on Epstein relationship
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick was on Capitol Hill on Wednesday to testify about his relationship with late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Antisemitic assaults reached record high in U.S. in 2025, ADL finds
item.description: Physical antisemitic assaults reached a record high in the U.S. in 2025, according to the Anti-Defamation League's annual audit on antisemitism. Oren Segal, Anti-Defamation League senior vice president of counter-extremism and intelligence, joins to unpack the findings.

item.title: Trump-backed challengers emerge largely victorious in Indiana GOP primaries
item.description: Tuesday was not a triumphant primary night for Indiana Republican lawmakers who broke with President Trump on redistricting. CBS News political director Fin Gómez has the details.

item.title: Where do potential Iran peace talks stand as Trump says there's progress?
item.description: President Trump insisted he is making progress on peace talks with Iran on Wednesday. He said the regime wants to make a deal and threatened to resume bombing the country if it doesn't accept. CBS News correspondent Natalie Brand reports on the latest.

item.title: Path to ending Iran war unclear as Trump and White House give mixed messaging
item.description: President Trump started Wednesday by threatening a "higher level" of strikes on Iran, but also said there is a very good chance of the war ending. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

item.title: Blanche: Obama's concerns about DOJ targeting Trump critics are "extraordinarily rich"
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said it's "extraordinarily rich" for former President Barack Obama to say he's concerned about the "politicization of the criminal justice system" under the Trump administration. "There's something extraordinarily rich about a Democrat suggesting that this administration is charging our political adversaries," Blanche told CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez.

item.title: Ted Turner, media tycoon who founded CNN, dies at age 87
item.description: Ted Turner built a media empire that included cable channels CNN, TBS and TNT, and he owned the Atlanta Braves for 20 years.

item.title: Autonomous cars to be ticketed for traffic violations in California
item.description: Autonomous cars in California will no longer be immune to traffic tickets once a new law takes effect in July. CNET editor-at-large Scott Stein joins CBS News to discuss this and other top technology stories of the day.

item.title: Suspect arrested in Oklahoma party shooting that killed 1, injured 22
item.description: Police said they have arrested an 18-year-old in connection with a shooting Sunday during a party that left one woman dead and 22 other people wounded.

item.title: FDA authorizes fruit-flavored vapes for the first time
item.description: For the first time, the FDA has authorized fruit-flavored electronic cigarettes intended for adult smokers. Some health groups and parent organizations worry it could drive more underage vaping. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: Kentucky Derby winner skipping Preakness Stakes, won't make Triple Crown run
item.description: The last time a horse won the Triple Crown was in 2018.

item.title: Manhunt on for retired special forces soldier accused of shooting wife
item.description: Authorities say Craig Berry, a retired special forces soldier, shot his wife and then fled into the woods in northern Tennessee.

item.title: Retired special forces soldier accused of shooting wife in Tennessee found dead
item.description: Craig Berry, 53, was found dead on Wednesday of an apparent gunshot wound, the Stewart County Sheriff's Office said.

item.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship
item.description: A hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and infected multiple others, officials said.

item.title: Suspect arrested after two fatal Carrollton shootings tied to business dispute, police say
item.description: Police say both shootings were connected and stemmed from a financial dispute between the suspect and the victims.

item.title: iPhone owners could get up to $95 from a new Apple settlement
item.description: A proposed $250 million settlement would compensate millions of consumers who bought the iPhone 16, iPhone 15 Pro or iPhone 15 Pro Max.

item.title: Fruit-flavored e-cigarettes for adults OK'd by FDA in major shift under Trump
item.description: The FDA announced its first authorization of fruit-flavored electronic cigarettes intended for adult smokers, a major policy shift after months of appeals to President Trump from the vaping industry.

item.title: Hantavirus possibly transmitted human to human on stricken cruise ship, WHO says
item.description: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.

item.title: Accused correspondents' dinner shooter charged with assaulting a federal officer
item.description: Accused White House Correspondents' Dinner attacker Cole Allen was indicted Tuesday on four charges, including a new charge of assaulting a federal officer.

item.title: What We Call The President
item.description: Mr. vs. President
